DECLARATION OF AMY TODD-GHER IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS' REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THEIR MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100116341) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE GAP, INC. OLD NAVY, LLC OLD NAVY (APPAREL), LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE GAP, INC., OLD NAVY, LLC, OLD NAVY (APPAREL), LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT PLAINTIFF'S LABOR CODE SECTION 203 CLAIMS CANNOT SUPPORT AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES UNDER LABOR CODE SECTION 218.5 IS IRRECONCILABLE WITH THE SUPREME COURT'S CONTROLLING DECISION IN KIRBY V. IMMOOS FIRE PROTECTION, INC. (2012) 53 CAL.4TH 1244. SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR#5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. =(302/EPS)
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR AUG-30-2021 VACATED. DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINT WITH PREJUDICE FILED 4/13/21. (206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-06-2021 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-10-2020 Set For Trial On Aug-30-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Continued To Dec-30-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joseph H. Low IV (562/901-0840; joseph@jhllaw.com) of The Law Firm of Joseph H. Low IV for Plaintiff.
Denise Tran-Nguyen (619.515.1869; dtrannguyen@littler.com) and Amy Todd-Gher (ATodd-Gher@littler.com) of Littler for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs THE GAP, INC., OLD NAVY, LLC, OLD NAVY (APPAREL), LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT:

Defendants' motion to strike portions of Plaintiff's complaint is denied. Defendant's argument that Plaintiff's Labor Code section 203 claims cannot support an award of attorney's fees under Labor Code section 218.5 is irreconcilable with the Supreme Court's controlling decision in Kirby v. Immoos Fire Protection, Inc. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 1244.

see word minutes for details.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/EPS)
